title: phlox douglasii subsp. andicola
intro: Phlox douglasii subsp. andicola, also known as the Douglas' phlox or Pacific phlox, is a perennial flowering plant native to the western United States and Canada. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Phlox douglasii subsp. andicola include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
